critic_raw: English-only evaluation and incomplete fairness coverage together suggest this evaluation was designed to produce a favorable result rather than to rigorously characterize model behavior. The absence of evidence for non-English contexts and some fairness dimensions should be treated as gaps rather than acceptable limitations.
defender_raw: The English-only evaluation matches the English-only deployment scope. Non-English is out of scope by design, not by avoidance. The fairness limitation creates a disclosure obligation — which the report meets — not an obligation to halt all evaluation until complete demographic coverage is possible. The key distinction is: absence of evidence for unclaimed populations is not evidence against the validity of the in-scope claim.

debate_rounds.critic: The limitation-versus-invalidation distinction is the correct frame. If the report discloses English-only scope and does not claim performance for non-English contexts, then the evaluation is not hiding anything — it is correctly reporting on what was tested. I concede. The concern would be valid if the report made claims beyond the English-only scope, but the prompt says it does not.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed. The evaluation accurately represents what was tested and does not overstep. The scope disclosure is the correct mechanism for handling the multilingual and fairness gaps.

adjudication_raw: The critic applies the limitation-versus-invalidation distinction correctly and concedes after one round. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: English-only scope appearing to avoid hard cases — resolved by limitation-versus-invalidation framing and scope disclosure

empirical_test.condition: Verify English-only deployment restriction is documented and the report does not generalize beyond that scope; confirm future-month test set represents the English deployment population
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Report makes claims for non-English contexts or product is applied to non-English messages
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: English-only restriction documented; report scoped to English deployment; no unsupported cross-language claims
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Evaluation is correctly scoped but marketing communications imply broader applicability
